metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen R7 AC3 to include either: (a) a defined review cadence (e.g., 'the README documents a weekly cadence and the responsible role'), or (b) an automated alert emission (make target or CI job that exits non-zero when any criterion exceeds 10%), so the demotion guarantee has a verifiable execution path.
evidence: Parent SC2: 'when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%... its registry posture is demoted to advisory by an ordinary criteria_routing.json change'
R7 AC3: 'The alarm rule (trailing blocking-FP proxy >10% -> demote to advisory) and response playbook are documented in docs/plan-review-gate.md'
R7 AC1: 'The instrumentation job is committed and re-runnable against the live store, emitting per-criterion trailing metrics documented in a README'
No child AC requires: automated alerting when threshold is breached, a defined review cadence, or a committed obligation to execute the criteria_routing.json change on breach
findings.finding: Parent SC2 guarantees that when a criterion's blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10% it 'is demoted to advisory by an ordinary criteria_routing.json change,' but no child owns the detection-and-execution of this demotion. R7 documents the alarm rule and response playbook, but R7's ACs require only that the rule is 'documented in docs/plan-review-gate.md' — no child commits to performing the criteria_routing.json edit, verifying the instrumentation fires an actionable alert, or defining a review cadence that guarantees the playbook is consulted. If R7's instrumentation runs but no operator checks it, the demotion guarantee silently lapses.
findings.location: child b1a4-af7a-4a48-465d: R7 — AC3 vs. Parent Success Criteria SC2
scenarios: R7 ships and emits metrics; no operator checks the job output for weeks; a criterion's FP proxy exceeds 10% and stays there; the parent SC2 guarantee is violated with no automated backstop
The response playbook is documented but the cadence ('run the job every N days and inspect output') is left unspecified, making compliance unverifiable at the epic completion gate
findings.suggested_fix: Add to R7's AC3: 'The README specifies a minimum review cadence (at minimum monthly) and the role responsible for executing the criteria_routing.json demotion edit when the threshold is breached; optionally, the make target exits non-zero when any criterion's trailing proxy exceeds 10% to enable CI alerting.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile R6 What and AC2: either restrict AC2's trigger to 'advisory findings only' (matching What), or extend What to describe what the coaching note emits when a blocking finding triggers it.
evidence: What: 'a deterministic coaching note that ranks the round's surviving advisories into apply-now vs defer-with-reason buckets using only recorded finding fields'
AC2: 'Blocking and advisory findings are both eligible triggers, and the note orders advisories by the documented ranking rule, pinned by test'
No description in What of what the move outputs when a blocking finding is the trigger; the ranking output covers only advisories
findings.finding: R6's What description and AC2 are contradictory on trigger scope: the What says the coaching move 'ranks the round's surviving advisories into apply-now vs defer-with-reason buckets' (advisories only), but AC2 states 'Blocking and advisory findings are both eligible triggers.' The What never describes what the move does when a blocking finding triggers it, leaving the AC-expanded behavior unspecified and unimplementable.
findings.location: child 5dac-c177-56ff-4af4: R6 — AC2 vs. What paragraph
scenarios: A developer implementing the move to satisfy the What builds an advisory-only trigger; the AC2 pinned test requires blocking-finding triggers and fails
A developer implementing to satisfy AC2 adds blocking triggers but has no spec for what the note says about blocking findings, producing an arbitrary output that the test cannot pin deterministically
findings.suggested_fix: If the intent is advisory-triage only (consistent with the Why rationale — 'authors defer advisory findings'), remove 'Blocking and' from AC2 so it reads 'Advisory findings are eligible triggers.' If blocking findings should also trigger a separate note section, add a sentence to What describing the blocking-finding output.

evidence: docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/: 5342-568c-b881-4b2a & a880-b7e1-dc3e-407c (no ordering edge)
docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/: 5342-568c-b881-4b2a & b1a4-af7a-4a48-465d (no ordering edge)

findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

evidence: 2 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
vague/subjective terms present: clean
findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default trailing window for the blocking-FP proxy (e.g. 'last N completed reviews, minimum M findings, or 30 calendar days — whichever is larger') so R7 implementors and reviewers can deterministically evaluate the demotion threshold.
evidence: 'when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10% (the blocking-FP proxy for a criterion is the fraction of that criterion's BLOCKING findings, over a trailing window of reviews, that are subsequently adjudicated false-positive / not-block-worthy'
The phrase 'trailing window of reviews' leaves both duration and minimum-sample-size undefined.
R7 child is said to carry details, but the Success Criteria at epic level cites the 10% rule as a governing deliverable condition — executor must operationalize it.
findings.finding: The 'trailing window' for the blocking-FP proxy (the denominator period over which the 10% demotion threshold is measured) is never defined — neither a time duration (e.g. 30 days) nor a review-count (e.g. 100 reviews) is stated. An executor implementing R7 instrumentation and the demotion logic cannot determine when the proxy has crossed 10% without knowing the window size.
findings.location: Success Criteria — bullet 2: 'when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%...'
scenarios: R7 implementor must pick an arbitrary window (e.g. last 20 reviews vs. last 6 months), leading to inconsistent demotion behavior.
Demotion criterion in Success Criteria is not fully evaluable in-session because the observable state ('>10% proxy') depends on an unspecified window.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Success Criteria bullet: 'trailing window = last 50 completed reviews (or 30 calendar days, whichever yields more data), minimum 10 BLOCKING findings for the criterion before the proxy is considered stable; R7's child AC is authoritative if more specific.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the trailing window for the blocking-FP proxy in the Success Criteria (e.g., 'last N completed reviews, minimum M criteria-blocking findings, or D calendar days — whichever yields the larger sample') so R7 implementors and reviewers can deterministically evaluate the 10% demotion threshold.
evidence: Plan text: 'over a trailing window of reviews' — no window size specified
No field in criteria_routing.json defines a window parameter (confirmed by codebase search for 'fp_demotion_threshold', 'trailing_window', 'posture_demotion' — all return no hits)
ABSENCE: no R7 child AC and no config file in the repo designates where the window value is persisted or what its default is
The plan explicitly intends demotion to be 'an ordinary criteria_routing.json change' (a config edit, not a code change), yet the threshold driving that decision has no configurable home
findings.finding: The blocking-FP proxy demotion decision rule in Success Criteria references 'a trailing window of reviews' but never defines the window size — no time duration (e.g. 30 days), no review count (e.g. last 50 completed reviews), and no minimum-sample-size floor. An executor implementing R7 instrumentation cannot determine when the proxy has crossed the 10% threshold without knowing the denominator period.
findings.location: Success Criteria — second bullet: 'when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10% (the blocking-FP proxy for a criterion is the fraction of that criterion's BLOCKING findings, over a trailing window of reviews, that are subsequently adjudicated false-positive / not-block-worthy...)'
scenarios: R7 implementors independently choose different window sizes (e.g., last 20 reviews vs. last 6 months), producing inconsistent demotion behavior across environments or implementors
A criterion with 2 blocking findings, 1 adjudicated FP, passes the 10% threshold on a short window but fails on a long window — demotion fires non-deterministically
The 'in-session evaluable' requirement (F1) for the Success Criterion cannot be met because the observable state depends on an unspecified window parameter
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Success Criteria bullet: 'trailing window = last 50 completed reviews for that criterion (minimum 10 blocking findings before the proxy is considered stable); R7's child story carries this as a configurable constant in the instrumentation schema.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out procedure (e.g., 'revert the Gerrit CL and re-run E5/the relevant gate to confirm clean') for review_kernel and criteria_routing.json changes, covering the window between ship and the first trailing-window demotion reading.
evidence: 'validated changes are incorporated DIRECTLY into the packaged review process (no config-flag or overlay gating - operator direction 2026-07-15)'
'src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/ (SHARED with code-review - R5 requires the E5 non-regression gate)' — a non-regression gate before ship does not substitute for a back-out path after ship
ABSENCE: no Gerrit-revert procedure, no rollback commit sequence, no describe-the-revert-steps language anywhere in the plan
findings.finding: The plan prohibits config-flag/overlay gating (operator direction 2026-07-15) and ships changes directly into the packaged registry, but provides no explicit rollback / back-out mechanism for review_kernel changes (shared with code-review) or criteria_routing.json changes if an acute regression appears after the eval gate passes. The demotion rule handles slow FP drift; it does not address a hard regression requiring rapid reversal.
findings.location: Context: 'Implementation targets: src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/ (SHARED with code-review — R5 requires the E5 non-regression gate)'; Success Criteria: 'no config-flag or overlay gating — operator direction 2026-07-15'; AC line 3
scenarios: R5 ships a review_kernel change that passes E5 in eval but causes a latent regression in code-review consumers discovered a week post-ship — there is no documented path to revert.

A criteria_routing.json change passes its gate but produces a blocking-FP spike above 10% before enough trailing-window data accumulates to trigger the demotion rule — the plan has no interim back-out path.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Context or a new 'Rollback Plan' section, add: 'If a post-ship regression is detected before the 10% demotion threshold fires, the responsible CL is reverted via Gerrit revert; the revert CL must pass the same gate(s) as the original before re-landing. For review_kernel changes, revert is confirmed clean by re-running E5 on the reverted commit.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC #3 (or add a dedicated AC) to require that any deferred experiment child includes a committed docs/experiments/ writeup documenting the re-run attempt (changed method described, re-run result recorded) per the INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE, or an explicit waiver if the first run was clearly failing (not merely inconclusive).
evidence: Context: 'an experiment that fails its own validity gate ... gets at most ONE documented re-run with changed method; if still not clearly passing, every dependent R-story closes deferred with the results recorded'
AC #3: 'closed/deferred with the failing result recorded' — covers the terminal state only
No AC requires a re-run attempt writeup or evidence that the changed-method re-run was performed before deferral
findings.finding: The INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE in Context requires a documented re-run with changed method before any dependent R-story is deferred, but no AC verifies that this re-run step was taken. AC #3 covers only the end state ('closed/deferred with the failing result recorded'), not the mandated intermediate step (one re-run with changed method, results documented).
findings.location: Context: INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE; Acceptance Criteria AC #3
scenarios: An inconclusive experiment is immediately closed/deferred without a re-run attempt, satisfying AC #3 (failing result is recorded) but violating the INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE — and no AC would catch the shortcut

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Designate a configurable home for the 10% blocking-FP demotion threshold (e.g., a `fp_demotion_threshold` field in criteria_routing.json or a named constant in the R7 config schema) so threshold tuning remains a config change, not a code change.
evidence: Plan states: '10% is the demotion threshold' inline in prose, without specifying where this value lives in the codebase
The plan explicitly designs posture changes as 'an ordinary criteria_routing.json change' — the threshold driving that decision should be equally configurable
Calibration-3 precedent (relishable-ammonitic-hoverfly) showed thresholds do evolve (T5e demoted on FP evidence), implying the 10% value may also need adjustment as the corpus matures
ABSENCE: no AC line, no R7 child story spec, and no config-file reference designates where this threshold is persisted
findings.finding: The 10% blocking-FP demotion threshold that governs live production posture changes is stated only in Success Criteria prose. No configurable home (e.g., a field in criteria_routing.json or a named config constant) is designated, risking it being hardcoded in R7's implementation and requiring a code change rather than a config change when the threshold needs tuning.
findings.location: Success Criteria — blocking-FP proxy paragraph: '10% is the demotion threshold'
scenarios: R7's implementation encodes `if fp_rate > 0.10` as a literal constant; when the ops team wants to tighten or loosen the demotion threshold based on observed corpus behavior, it requires a code change, review cycle, and deploy rather than a config edit.

A future calibration pass finds 10% too permissive for a high-stakes criterion but too strict for a noisy one — per-criterion configurability is impossible if the value is a single hardcoded constant.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a top-level or per-criterion `fp_demotion_threshold` field to criteria_routing.json (defaulting to 0.10), read by R7's monitoring logic. Document its semantics in docs/plan-review-gate.md alongside the posture-demotion procedure.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Ground the 10% blocking-FP proxy demotion threshold in prior art or derive it from the existing adjudication corpus (e.g., what FP rate the current blocking criteria show on the plan-v2/v3 calibration dataset), and specify the trailing window size.
evidence: Plan states: '10% is the demotion threshold' with no derivation or citation
T5e precedent cited was data-driven (calibration replay), not threshold-triggered
No prior FP-proxy monitoring system found in docs/experiments/ or src/rebar/llm/plan_review/
The existing calibration process (docs/experiments/plan-review-threshold-calibration.md) uses adjudication replay, not a running proxy metric
findings.finding: The 10% blocking-FP proxy demotion threshold — 'when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%... its registry posture is demoted to advisory' — is stated as a design rule with no prior-art citation or derivation. The plan cites T5e's demotion (calibration 3) as a precedent, but that demotion was evidence-driven from observed data (validity 0.391, 59% verifier-drop rate), not from a pre-set threshold being breached. No comparable monitoring system in the existing codebase or cited literature establishes 10% as the correct sensitivity point for this signal.
findings.location: Success Criteria: 'when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10% (the **blocking-FP proxy**...) its registry posture is demoted'
scenarios: At 10%, a criterion that blocks 100 reviews and generates 10 FP adjudications gets demoted — this may be too sensitive if most criteria have low volume and high variance.
The window size ('trailing window') is undefined; a short window could trigger spurious demotions from a cluster of edge-case tickets.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the R5 IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump coordination protocol: name who initiates (e.g. 'R5 author checks the floor-split story's current version at claim time and opens a shared bump PR if one is not already open'), and state the fallback if the floor-split story has already landed its bump.
evidence: 'coordinated at claim time' names no owner, no artifact, and no protocol.
No fallback is defined if the floor-split story (large-sleepful-needlefish) bumps before R5 is claimed or if both are claimed simultaneously.
The phrase 'never two half-bumps' implies a hard constraint but provides no mechanism to enforce it.
findings.finding: The R5 IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump coordination mechanism is described only as 'coordinated at claim time with the floor-split story's own potential bump' — no named owner, no handshake protocol, and no fallback if the floor-split story has already bumped before R5 is claimed. This reads as a placeholder delegating a real design decision ('figure out coordination') to the executor.
findings.location: Context section — 'R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump is coordinated at claim time with the floor-split story's own potential bump so the corpus segments cleanly (one bump train, never two half-bumps).'
scenarios: Two engineers claim R5 and the floor-split story concurrently; neither knows who initiates the bump coordination, resulting in a race condition or a skipped bump.
The floor-split story closes before R5 is claimed, leaving the executor with no guidance on whether R5 should initiate its own bump or defer.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'outcome corpus' in AC #1 (or Context) — clarify what it contains (e.g., ticket-claim outcome labels from the §5 dataset), how it differs from the finding-adjudication corpus, and what inter-rater kappa applies to each.
evidence: AC #1 text: 'inter-rater kappa >= 0.7 recorded' paired with 'outcome corpus + finding-adjudication corpus'
Success Criteria uses 'finding-adjudication ground-truth corpus' — no mention of 'outcome corpus'
Context uses 'finding-adjudication ground-truth corpus' — no mention of 'outcome corpus'
The report reference (§5: 'post-claim description edits on 16/505 claimed work tickets') suggests a ticket-outcome corpus may be intended, but this is inferred, not stated
findings.finding: The term 'outcome corpus' in AC #1 is used nowhere else in the plan; Context and Success Criteria reference only the 'finding-adjudication ground-truth corpus' (or 'finding-adjudication corpus'). An implementer cannot determine from the plan what the 'outcome corpus' contains, how it is structured, or whether it is the same artifact as or distinct from the finding-adjudication corpus.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC #1: 'outcome corpus + finding-adjudication corpus'
scenarios: Implementer produces only a finding-adjudication corpus for E1, omitting the outcome corpus entirely
Two team members produce different artifacts, both calling their work the 'outcome corpus'
findings.suggested_fix: In AC #1, replace 'outcome corpus + finding-adjudication corpus' with two explicitly named corpora: (a) 'claim-outcome corpus (ticket-level: was the claimed capability actually present — drawn from the §5 16/505 dataset)' and (b) 'finding-adjudication corpus (finding-level: per-finding block/advisory/FP labels)'. State kappa is measured separately for each.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or augment AC #3's R5 gate terms) that verifies the IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump for R5 was coordinated with the floor-split story — e.g., 'R5's claim records a single coordinated IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump shared with the floor-split story, with no intervening half-bumps.'
evidence: Context describes the coordination as a deliverable constraint with a clear correctness condition ('corpus segments cleanly, one bump train, never two half-bumps')
AC #3 covers R5 landing or deferral but says nothing about bump coordination
No other AC references IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION
findings.finding: The IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump coordination for R5 — described in Context as a concrete sequencing/coordination constraint ('R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump is coordinated at claim time with the floor-split story's own potential bump so the corpus segments cleanly (one bump train, never two half-bumps)') — has no covering AC. No acceptance criterion verifies this coordination was performed.
findings.location: Context: 'R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump is coordinated at claim time...'
scenarios: R5 ships with its own bump while the floor-split story also bumps independently, producing two half-bumps and a split corpus — exactly the defect the constraint was written to prevent, but nothing in the ACs would catch it

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assign the INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE and the blocking-FP proxy measurement methodology (trailing window, adjudication corpus linkage, 10% threshold) to a specific section in docs/plan-review-gate.md (or a new ADR), and reference that doc from the relevant AC lines so the contracts are discoverable after ticket close.
evidence: The INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE is described only in Context prose with no assignment to a doc artifact.
The blocking-FP proxy definition (trailing window, adjudication corpus, 10% threshold) is described only in Success Criteria prose.

AC line 4 says 'docs/plan-review-gate.md updated to match' but does not explicitly require the measurement methodology or the inconclusive-gate rule to appear there — the update requirement is scoped to 'landed changes', not to the operating rules governing future experiments.
findings.finding: The INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE and the blocking-FP proxy measurement methodology (trailing window definition, adjudication corpus linkage, 10% threshold) are new operational contracts described only in the plan's Context and Success Criteria prose. They are not assigned to any specific persistent documentation artifact, so implementers landing future R-story follow-ons or operators tuning posture will have no authoritative reference after this ticket closes.
findings.location: Context: 'INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE: an experiment that fails its own validity gate…'; Success Criteria: 'the blocking-FP proxy for a criterion is the fraction…measured by R7's instrumentation against E1's committed finding-adjudication ground-truth corpus; 10% is the demotion threshold'
scenarios: A future experiment child is written by a different author who does not have this ticket in context — no authoritative source defines what 'INCONCLUSIVE' means or how to count re-runs.
The ops team wants to audit the 10% demotion decisions — no doc links the trailing-window methodology to the instrumentation R7 ships.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC line: 'docs/plan-review-gate.md §Experiment-Validity-Rules documents the INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE (one re-run maximum, defer on ambiguity) and the blocking-FP proxy definition (trailing window, adjudication corpus, 10% demotion threshold) before R2/R3/R4 land.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that R4's necessity/no-op probe and lightweight bug review tier share the same eval gate and cannot ship independently; if they can each deliver value alone, split them into two leaf children with explicit gate assignments.
evidence: Child title contains an explicit structural 'and': 'necessity/no-op probe + lightweight bug review tier'

A probe (detection logic, likely in passes.py or criteria_routing.json) and a new review tier (a distinct registry/rubric artifact) are heterogeneous change kinds that could each ship and deliver value independently
The plan's Context section does not explain why these two concerns must travel together in a single leaf child
findings.finding: Child R4's title bundles two potentially independently-releasable concerns: (1) a necessity/no-op probe (a detection/scoring mechanism) and (2) a lightweight bug review tier (a structural registry change). If the bug review tier can ship and deliver value independently of the probe, this violates single-concern at the leaf level.
findings.location: Child ticket: clerkish-cloggy-cod — R4: necessity/no-op probe + lightweight bug review tier (gated retrospective)
scenarios: If the no-op probe experiment gates the probe but not the tier, or vice versa, the combined child cannot close cleanly
A reviewer implementing only the tier or only the probe would have a releasable increment — the parts are not individually worthless apart

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the informal 'coordinate at claim time' convention for the R5 IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump with an enforced mechanism (e.g., declare R5 as a hard sequencing dep on the floor-split story's bump commit, or designate one story as the sole bump owner and make the other a no-bump consumer), so the 'one bump train, never two half-bumps' invariant is structurally guaranteed rather than coordination-dependent.
evidence: 'coordinated at claim time' is an informal convention, not a declared sequencing dependency or atomic guard
The plan names the failure mode ('never two half-bumps') but specifies no enforcement mechanism to prevent it
src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py line 35: IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION = 'plan-v3' — corpus segmentation depends on this version being bumped exactly once per formula change
R5 and the floor-split story (large-sleepful-needlefish) are described as running alongside each other with no hard ordering dep between them on this specific bump
findings.finding: The R5 IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump coordination is stated as 'coordinated at claim time' with the floor-split story, but this is an informal temporal convention with no enforced sequencing mechanism. If both stories are claimed concurrently or in the wrong order, two independent bump commits can occur in the same corpus window — exactly the 'two half-bumps' failure the plan names as the risk to avoid. The codebase confirms IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION = 'plan-v3' in sidecar.py; a split bump would mix formula cohorts in a single version label, corrupting the segmented calibration replay that depends on clean version boundaries.
findings.location: Context section: 'R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump is coordinated at claim time with the floor-split story's own potential bump so the corpus segments cleanly (one bump train, never two half-bumps)'
scenarios: R5 is claimed first and bumps IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION to 'plan-v4'; the floor-split story is then claimed and also bumps to a different value (e.g. 'plan-v4a') — two cohorts in the same sidecar stream, neither cleanly segmented.
Both stories are claimed in the same time window by different agents; each independently bumps the version in separate commits that arrive in rapid succession, splitting the corpus mid-calibration.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit dep: R5 declares the floor-split story's bump as a prerequisite (R5 blocked-by floor-split on this specific commit), or designate the floor-split story as the sole IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION owner and specify that R5 inherits the bumped version rather than issuing its own bump.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Commit `docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md` (or a stable permalink to commit `143d5074e`) into the repo so that R1-R7 gate terms can be audited against the source experiment table by any reviewer of the child stories.
evidence: docs/research/ directory does not exist in the repo snapshot
Ticket englacial-communal-conure/d39b not found via show_ticket
Plan states: 'GATE TERMS: each child R1-R7 story carries its own falsifiable pass/fail thresholds (drawn from the report's section-6 experiment table)'
ABSENCE: no file matching task-decomposition-sota found under any docs/ path
findings.finding: The foundational SOTA research document (docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md, commit 143d5074e) — the primary prior-art basis for all R1-R7 recommendations and their gate-term numbers — is absent from the repo and inaccessible via any tool. The plan's gate terms are 'drawn from the report's section-6 experiment table,' but that table cannot be independently navigated or verified. If the doc exists only in a commit not present in the snapshot, reviewers and implementers cannot audit whether R1-R7 faithfully implement the report's recommendations or whether the thresholds are correctly transcribed.
findings.location: Context section: 'Integrate the task-decomposition SOTA research (docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md, commit 143d5074e, ticket englacial-communal-conure/d39b)'
scenarios: An implementer claiming an R-story cannot verify their gate-term numbers against the source table — they can only trust the child ticket's ACs, with no audit path back to the report.
A reviewer of an R-child story cannot cross-check that the threshold (e.g., kappa >= 0.7 for E1) is what the SOTA report actually recommended.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `## Prior-art source` section in this epic's Context that either (a) confirms the SOTA doc is committed at the cited commit and navigable at that path, or (b) includes a verbatim excerpt of §6's experiment table so the gate terms are self-contained in the epic.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a concrete enforcement mechanism for the IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION one-bump-train rule (e.g., a pre-merge CI check that rejects a bump if another open story already carries one, or a sequencing dependency that gates R5's claim on the floor-split story's close).
evidence: IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION = 'plan-v3' is a single constant in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py:35
No locking or claim-coordination mechanism found for version-bump sequencing in the codebase
Plan uses passive voice: 'is coordinated' — no agent or mechanism named
The floor-split story (large-sleepful-needlefish) is a concurrent track with its own potential bump
findings.finding: The plan asserts that R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump 'is coordinated at claim time with the floor-split story's own potential bump so the corpus segments cleanly (one bump train, never two half-bumps)' — but provides no mechanism for how this coordination is enforced. Two concurrent stories that both might bump IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION have no specified locking, ordering gate, or pre-merge check to prevent simultaneous bumps. This is an unverified capability assertion: the plan claims the coordination 'is' done without specifying how.
findings.location: Context section: 'R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump is coordinated at claim time with the floor-split story's own potential bump so the corpus segments cleanly (one bump train, never two half-bumps)'
scenarios: R5 and the floor-split story are claimed concurrently; both bump IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION in parallel branches; the merge produces a version collision or one bump silently overwrites the other.
One story's bump lands first; the second story's calibration replay segments against the wrong version boundary.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit dependency: R5 cannot be claimed until large-sleepful-needlefish is closed (or vice versa), enforced via the ticket dependency graph, eliminating the need for any runtime coordination.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'outcome corpus' in AC #1 (or the Context section): specify what it contains (e.g., ticket-level claim-outcome labels drawn from the §5 16/505 dataset), how it differs from the finding-adjudication corpus, and which inter-rater kappa measurement applies to each corpus.
evidence: AC #1 text: 'E1 ground-truth corpora are frozen and committed under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/ with a versioned README (outcome corpus + finding-adjudication corpus, inter-rater kappa >= 0.7 recorded)'
Context section uses only 'finding-adjudication ground-truth corpus': 'E1's finding-adjudication ground-truth corpus'
Success Criteria uses only 'finding-adjudication ground-truth corpus': 'R7's instrumentation against E1's committed finding-adjudication ground-truth corpus'
ABSENCE: the term 'outcome corpus' does not appear in any other section of the plan, the codebase (confirmed via search), or the existing docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/ README
The §5 source data (16/505 ticket post-claim edits) suggests a ticket-outcome corpus may be intended, but this is inferred, not stated anywhere in the plan
findings.finding: The term 'outcome corpus' appears in AC #1 ('outcome corpus + finding-adjudication corpus, inter-rater kappa >= 0.7 recorded') but is never defined anywhere in the plan. The rest of the plan consistently uses only 'finding-adjudication ground-truth corpus' or 'finding-adjudication corpus'. An executor building E1 cannot determine what the outcome corpus contains, its schema/structure, or whether it is the same artifact as or distinct from the finding-adjudication corpus.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC #1: 'outcome corpus + finding-adjudication corpus, inter-rater kappa >= 0.7 recorded'
scenarios: An E1 implementer produces only a finding-adjudication corpus (since that is what the rest of the plan describes) and omits the outcome corpus entirely, leaving AC #1 technically unfulfilled without knowing it
Two raters produce inconsistent 'outcome corpus' artifacts because the term's content, schema, and inter-rater scoring dimension are undefined — kappa cannot be computed consistently

The R7 measurement mechanism (which references 'E1's committed finding-adjudication ground-truth corpus') cannot determine whether the outcome corpus feeds into the measurement or is a separate artifact
findings.suggested_fix: In AC #1, replace 'outcome corpus + finding-adjudication corpus' with two explicitly named corpora: (a) 'claim-outcome corpus (ticket-level: was the asserted capability present — drawn from the §5 16/505 dataset, labeled block-present/absent)' and (b) 'finding-adjudication corpus (finding-level: per-finding block/advisory/FP labels used by R7 measurement)'. State that kappa >= 0.7 is measured separately for each and record both values.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a fallback state for R7 when E1 closes deferred: e.g., 'R7 operates in corpus-less data-collection mode (records BLOCKING findings and adjudication signals) but does not compute or surface FP rates until a valid E1 corpus is committed; the demotion logic is gated on corpus availability.'
evidence: Plan text: 'first wave after E1 is R7 + R6 (instrumentation and the no-gate move), so dogfood monitoring exists before any detection change lands'

Plan text: 'INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE: an experiment that fails its own validity gate...gets at most ONE documented re-run...if still not clearly passing, every dependent R-story closes deferred with the results recorded'
Plan text (Success Criteria): 'measured by R7's instrumentation against E1's committed finding-adjudication ground-truth corpus' — R7's measurement function is explicitly coupled to E1's corpus

ABSENCE: no plan section specifies what R7 does when it is shipped but the E1 corpus is absent (e.g., operate in corpus-less collection mode, emit a degraded/uncalibrated metric, or gate its own activation on E1's completion)
findings.finding: R7 (instrumentation) is specified to ship in the first wave — before any detection change lands — and depends on E1's committed finding-adjudication ground-truth corpus to measure blocking-FP rates. However, the INCONCLUSIVE-GATE rule says that if E1 is inconclusive after one re-run, 'every dependent R-story closes deferred with the results recorded.' No fallback is specified for the half-shipped state where R7 is already in production but the E1 corpus it is supposed to measure against does not exist (E1 closed deferred).
findings.location: Context section — ORDERING paragraph: 'first wave after E1 is R7 + R6'; INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE: 'every dependent R-story closes deferred'; Success Criteria: 'R7's instrumentation against E1's committed finding-adjudication ground-truth corpus'
scenarios: E1 runs, fails its kappa gate, re-runs once, still fails — closes deferred per the INCONCLUSIVE-GATE rule. R7 is already deployed and collecting data, but has no adjudication corpus to measure against. The blocking-FP proxy defined in Success Criteria cannot be computed — demotion decisions cannot be made.
R7 silently emits uncalibrated or undefined FP metrics because no corpus is available, misleading operators into thinking the monitoring is functioning when it is not.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align R4's AC1 threshold for unnecessary-mechanism mutants with E2's pre-registration: either have R4 reference 'the E2 pre-registered threshold for class-4' instead of hardcoding 8/10, or have E2 explicitly pre-register 8/10 for that class in a cross-referenced commit.
evidence: R4 AC1: 'fires on 5886's plan fixture (pinned as a test) and on >=8/10 E2 unnecessary-mechanism mutants with <=1/10 controls flagged'
E2 AC2: 'Thresholds are pre-registered in the README in a commit that precedes any detector run against the corpus (verifiable by commit order)'
E2 has no reference to R4's 8/10 threshold; R4 has no reference to E2's pre-registration process; the thresholds can diverge
findings.finding: R4's retrospective gate hardcodes a threshold of '>=8/10 E2 unnecessary-mechanism mutants with <=1/10 controls flagged' (AC1), but E2's story owns the pre-registration of per-class thresholds and sets them independently in its README commit. Neither child cross-references the other to guarantee the thresholds match. If E2 pre-registers a different threshold (e.g., >=7/10) for the unnecessary-mechanism class, R4's gate row and E2's pre-registered threshold are contradictory, and R4's gate could neither clearly pass nor fail against E2's labeled corpus.
findings.location: child 03a9-e6d1-4acc-435d: R4 — AC1 vs. child d125-b4ce-c57f-4f06: E2 — AC2 (pre-registration)
scenarios: E2 pre-registers >=7/10 for class-4 (unnecessary-mechanism) based on expected difficulty; R4 gates on >=8/10; the probe achieves 7/10 — R4's gate fails but E2's pre-registered threshold would pass, creating an unresolvable adjudication conflict
E2 pre-registers >=9/10 for class-4; R4 achieves 8/10 — R4's own AC says it passes but E2's pre-registered threshold says it fails, blocking R4's landing without a clear owner of the final threshold
findings.suggested_fix: Change R4 AC1 to read '>=8/10 E2 unnecessary-mechanism mutants (pre-registered threshold for class-4 in E2's README)' and add a note in E2's README template that class-4's pre-registered threshold is 8/10, making both children point to the same number with E2 as the authoritative source.

findings.finding: The Success Criteria specifies an operational demotion rule — 'when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted to advisory' — but 'blocking-FP proxy' is never defined anywhere in the plan, codebase, documentation, or experiment harnesses. No formula, no computation window, no script computes this metric. The existing tooling measures contamination_rate (chunk co-residence), and calibration replays report rev_rr/validity/p90 — none of which is called 'blocking-FP proxy'. The R7 dogfood-monitoring story cannot be implemented without knowing what to measure.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'new criteria enter at advisory POSTURE first...with R7 dogfood monitoring: when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted to advisory'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to R7's AC: 'blocking-FP proxy = (number of block findings dropped by the author's immediate next re-review) / (total block findings in the review), measured as a trailing 20-review moving average per criterion, computed by scripts/plan_review_blocking_fp_rate.py reading REVIEW_RESULT sidecars.' Then add that field to sidecar.build_payload or a companion analysis script.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
